definition: The Spanish past tense used for completed actions with a definite endpoint (e.g. "ayer comí")
The Spanish past tense used for ongoing or habitual past actions and background description (e.g. "cuando era joven")
A verbal mood used to express doubt
Ser describes permanent qualities and identity; "estar" describes temporary states and location
The direct object of a sentence; often replaced by pronouns lo
The indirect object; replaced by pronouns me
column7: or les — indicates to whom or for whom

definition: Por often means "because of" or "through"; "para" often means "in order to" or "destined for"
The Spanish equivalent of "-ing" form (e.g. "hablando"); used with estar to form progressive tenses
